reason: Leaf-roll gall midge spends entire life cycle in Robinia canopy with no flower-foraging phase.
Foliage-feeding caterpillars live in the canopy and remain sedentary by day; not flower-foragers.
Geometrid caterpillars are canopy-bound foliage feeders with no ground or flower-visiting stage.
Brimstone moth and grey dagger caterpillars feed in the canopy and rest motionless during the day.
Orthosia moth caterpillars are canopy foliage-feeders on Salix with no pollinator role.
Mason and mining bees are classic spring pollinators that bob between blooms on Cercis and Prunus.
Hackberry psyllids are phloem-feeders permanently settled inside galls on Celtis foliage.
Large walnut aphid colonies are sedentary on Juglans leaf veins with no flower or ground phase.
Zelkova gall aphid lives inside leaf-fold galls on Zelkova with no external movement visible.
Leafminer moth larvae are confined inside Robinia leaf tissue throughout their development.
Blue tit
column8: and long-tailed tit are classic canopy insectivores that flit between trees.

reason: Lecanium scale adults are permanently fixed to bark surface under waxy covers; not flower-visitors.
Great tit and blue tit are archetypal Amsterdam canopy birds that flit actively between trees.
Observation context is roosting and cavity use in Pterocarya; idle-only suits resting state better than tree-flitter.
House sparrow (wall-and-roof urban-walker) and great tit (tree-canopy tree-flitter) need dual tags.
House sparrow and wood pigeon recorded as roosting in Catalpa and Paulownia canopy; idle-only suits roosting context.
House and tree sparrows are building-nesting pavement foragers; wall-and-roof and ground-urban with urban-walker.
Aphid parasitoid wasp hunts Cedrus canopy for aphid hosts; not a flower-forager.
Coal tit is a conifer-canopy specialist that flits energetically between needle-bearing trees.
Cypress bark beetle bores beneath bark of Metasequoia; detected by exit holes not surface activity.
Scolytine bark beetle spends entire life cycle inside Metasequoia bark; no visible surface presence.
Woolly beech aphid colonies are sedentary on Fagus leaf undersides with no flower-visiting role.
Arborvitae weevil is a slow-moving foliage-feeder on Thuja canopy; not a pollinator.
Leafminer micro-moth larvae develop entirely inside Castanea leaf tissue; entirely sedentary.
Gracillariid leafminers live inside Carpinus leaves; no flower-visiting role at any observed stage.
Holly leaf miner larva is enclosed within Ilex leaf lamina throughout development; does not move.
Root and crown pathogen infects Chamaecyparis belowground; tree-rooted overrides default tree-bark.
Microscopic eriophyid mite lives inside Corylus buds; tree-canopy overrides default anywhere.
Magpie and carrion crow are urban omnivore corvids that alternate tree perching and pavement foraging.
